item.title: Spring’s Hot-to-Cold Swings Are Being Juiced by Climate Change
item.description: Abrupt changes in temperature are getting spikier as the planet warms.

item.title: Spring Temperature Whiplash Is Getting Worse With Climate Change
item.description: Abrupt jumps from chilly to warm and back again are typical of the season but becoming more extreme in a warming world.

item.title: BMO Turns to AI and Quantum Computing to Predict Earthquakes
item.description: Bank of Montreal is betting on artificial intelligence and quantum computing to help predict earthquakes and respond to wildfires as part of the Canadian bank’s expansion in the US.

item.title: Nuclear AI Startup Fermi Promised Land and Ample Power. But It Couldn't Sign a Single Client
item.description: Now the ex-CEO is waging a battle for Fermi’s future and its vision of atomic-powered data centers in the Texas panhandle.

item.title: India Braces for Another Summer of Dangerous Heat
item.description: The country is already dealing with energy shortages caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: Warm, Wild Weather Puts Europe on Storm and Drought Watch in May
item.description: Europe is set for weeks of erratic and increasingly warm weather over the coming month, raising the risk of storms, early season drought stress and increasing cooling demand.

item.title: India’s Heat Exposes a Fragile Grid as Energy Crunch Deepens
item.description: India is likely to endure a blistering summer until monsoon rains arrive in June, with higher-than-average heat already straining power grids at a time when the country is grappling with energy shortages.

item.title: Texas Hill Country on Alert for Flooding as Heavy Rains Approach
item.description: A band of severe storms bearing down on central Texas has raised concerns about flooding, with heavy rains expected near the site of last year’s deadly Fourth of July floods.

item.title: Gresham House Said to Plan €1 Billion Fund for Battery Storage
item.description: Gresham House plans to raise a fund targeting roughly €1 billion ($1.2 billion) to give investors access to the growing market for battery-storage systems, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: How Countries Are Trying to Move Away From Fossil fuels
item.description: Nearly 60 nations, save the largest polluters, gathered in Colombia this week to discuss the energy transition.

item.title: Countries Draw Up Next Steps to Decarbonize at Colombia Fossil-Fuel Summit
item.description: The conference in Santa Marta ended on an optimistic note, with plans for a second meeting in 2027.

item.title: Hedge Fund Manager ‘Ignoring Trump’ Nets 39% Gain on Energy Bets
item.description: A hedge fund manager focused on navigating the energy transition has generated a 39% return so far this year, after adopting a policy of ignoring US President Donald Trump’s signals on the Iran war.

item.title: A New World Order Is Emerging. Can it Tackle Climate Change?
item.description: Gordon LaForge makes the case that, even as consensus falls apart on global problems, smaller groups will cooperate on international issues out of self interest.

item.title: Iran War Is ‘Supercharging’ the Energy Transition, UNFCCC Says
item.description: The Iran war is accelerating a shift toward renewable energy as governments race to reduce supply-chain vulnerabilities, according to UNFCCC Executive Secretary Simon Stiell.

item.title: China’s Embattled Solar Makers Turn to Batteries for Growth
item.description: China’s biggest maker of solar equipment wants to grow its batteries business to a similar size.

item.title: EDP Bets Big on Australia in $1 Billion Renewables Push in Asia
item.description: EDP SA’s renewables unit will invest about $1 billion in green energy projects across Asia-Pacific through 2028, with a focus on developing its presence in Australia.

item.title: Dutch Climate Minister Says Talks to Quit Fossil Fuels Have Momentum
item.description: Countries that move away from oil and gas will be “shielding their economies against the kind of price shocks that we’re seeing currently,” said Stientje van Veldhoven.

item.title: Atome Sees War Driving Shifts That Boost Low-Carbon Fertilizer
item.description: The low-carbon fertilizer industry is getting a boost as buyers shift to long-term contracts amid rising geopolitical risks, according to the top executive at Atome PLC.

item.title: How Global Deforestation Declined 36% in 2025
item.description: The world still lost an area of rainforest roughly the size of Denmark

item.title: France Unveils Fossil-Fuel Exit Plan at Colombia Climate Summit
item.description: The country aims to exit coal by 2030, oil by 2045 and natural gas by 2050.

item.title: Doubling of Australia’s Battery Capacity Cuts Reliance on Gas
item.description: Batteries are helping cut Australia’s reliance on natural gas and lowering electricity costs, according to the main grid’s operator.

item.title: Blackstone to Invest €2 Billion in Renewables Developer Eurowind
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has agreed to invest as much as €2 billion ($2.3 billion) in Eurowind Energy, a pan-European renewables developer and independent power producer.

item.title: Global Forest Loss Fell Sharply in 2025 After Hitting Record High
item.description: Clearing of the world’s tropical forests, whether for farmland or by wildfires, declined by 36% last year, according to the Global Forest Watch initiative.

item.title: Mirova Green Fund Exits Philippine Debt After Corruption Scandal
item.description: Mirova SA’s flagship green bond fund exited its position in Philippine debt following a corruption scandal that raised concerns investors may have inadvertently financed flood-control projects now under investigation for graft.

item.title: Chinese Wind Firm Ming Yang Doubles Down on Europe After UK Snub
item.description: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd., a major Chinese maker of offshore wind turbines, aims to finalize a European site for a factory by June, after the UK rejected its plan to build a plant in Scotland.

item.title: Unreported Coal Mine Methane Imperils Climate Action, Ember Says
item.description: Nations that fail to report coal mine methane emissions could be missing out on cheap opportunities to slash releases of the potent greenhouse gas, according to a report from energy think tank Ember.

item.title: Red-State AGs Call Out Credit-Rating Companies Over ESG
item.description: A group of state attorneys general wrote to the US Securities and Exchange Commission and major credit-rating firms, raising concerns about the use of environmental, social and governance factors in downgrade decisions.

item.title: China Solar Giant Longi Says First-Quarter Loss Widened
item.description: Chinese solar giant Longi Green Energy Technology Co. saw its net loss widened in the first quarter, despite stronger module pricing and exports offset overcapacity in the sector.

item.title: Hungarian Nuclear Plant to Cut Block 2 Capacity 50% for Repairs
item.description: Hungary’s nuclear energy authority said output at one block of the country’s sole atomic power station will be cut by 50% from April 29 for repairs on a generator support system.

item.title: From the UK to South Korea, Consumers Turn to EVs as Oil Prices Spike
item.description: March data reveals surge in EV sales from the UK to South Korea
